levels.description: Ad hoc security policy and roles are undefined.
levels.outcome: Security is reactive and not aligned to business objectives.

levels.description: Basic security policies exist, with limited oversight.
levels.outcome: Some controls are defined, but ownership is inconsistent.

levels.description: Formal governance processes are established and repeatable.
levels.outcome: Security decisions are documented and reviewed.

levels.description: Governance is integrated across business units with metrics.
Governance is optimized, continuously improving, and benchmarked.
levels.outcome: Security is a business enabler and measured proactively.

levels.description: Risk is identified informally and inconsistently.
levels.outcome: Potential threats are not systematically assessed.

levels.description: Basic risk assessments are completed for key assets.
levels.outcome: Some risks are tracked, but mitigation is ad hoc.

levels.description: Risk management processes are defined and repeatable.
levels.outcome: Risk registers and treatment plans are maintained.

levels.description: Risk decisions are driven by quantitative and qualitative data.
levels.outcome: Risk posture is reviewed regularly by leadership.

levels.description: Risk management is embedded across programs and continuously monitored.
levels.outcome: Risks are proactively reduced through strategic controls.

levels.description: Incident response is informal with no documented plan.
A basic incident response plan exists and is occasionally used.
levels.outcome: Incidents are handled, but lessons learned are limited.

levels.description: IR playbooks and communication plans are defined.
Response workflows are tested, measured, and improved.
levels.outcome: Recovery times are reduced and stakeholders are informed promptly.

levels.description: Incident response is mature with automated detection and adaptive learning.
levels.outcome: The organization recovers rapidly and evolves from every event.
